identity.role: Exact E8 lattice membership, roots, and closure receipts

pattern: complete-unformalized work -> formalization in paper-bound space -> Forge

source_work.state_before: E8 structure split across lfai/cona_e8.py (self-declared surrogate legality check used by ingress and the OctetExecutor 8-lane) and src/cmplx/lattice/e8.py (real root system, numpy-bound, self-trusting); never formalized, no forge

adjudicated_divergence: the lfai surrogate's conditions (sum even, sum of squares even, sum of squares mod 8 == 0) are provably NOT E8 membership: the mod-8 condition rejects all 112 integer roots (+-1,+-1,0^6) whose sum of squares is 2, and the integer-only API cannot represent the 128 half-integer roots — verified as check 10, every octet that passed through lfai ingress was judged by a non-E8 gate
e8.py's float/numpy norms replaced by exact doubled-integer coordinates (u = 2v): membership is u all-even or all-odd with sum(u) == 0 mod 4
lfai/ledger_merkle.py chain logic is sound but hardcodes /mnt/data; ledger receipts remain ChromaForge territory
validated_claims: Exactly 240 roots, split 112 integer + 128 half-integer, no duplicates.
Every root is an E8 member of norm^2 = 2.
Half-integer roots have an even number of minus signs.
Root inner products lie in {-2,-1,0,1,2}; -2 only at antipodes.
The lattice closes under root addition (all 57,600 pairs).
Root pair sums are even vectors with norm^2 in {0,2,4,6,8}.
The minimum norm^2 is 2 — no E8 vector of norm^2 = 1 (exhaustive in doubled coordinates).
An algorithmically derived simple system of 8 roots has Cartan diagonal 2, off-diagonal in {0,-1}, determinant 1, and degree sequence [1,1,1,2,2,2,2,3] — the E8 Dynkin diagram.
The CMPLXUNI surrogate rejects all 112 integer roots — it is not E8 membership.
honesty_boundary: The Weyl order is verified as an arithmetic identity, not by group enumeration. Unimodularity is implied by the Cartan determinant but deserves a basis-level Gram receipt. Sphere-packing optimality (Viazovska) is cited mathematics, not claimed by this verifier.
open_obligations: Chamber-counting receipt for the Weyl order.
Basis-level Gram determinant receipt for unimodularity.
Patch CMPLXUNI lfai ingress/octet to call E8Forge exact membership instead of the surrogate (upstream fix in the source repo).
Leech lattice constants in CMPLX (kissing number 196560) as the next profile-repo candidate.
notes: Sixth instance of the promotion pattern; first from the profile repo lane (user directive 2026-06-12: focus on profile repos next). The surrogate finding matters operationally: CMPLXUNI's LFAI ingress and OctetExecutor judged octet legality with a gate that rejects every true integer E8 root.
